commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f9008ec6e6e5a4aa132a4393463b06da7aac3632)
commands.review_summary:
This PR trims blank `logging.file` values to the default log path, adds a one-time stderr warning when file appends fail, and adds validation/tests for empty `logging.file`.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +102. Total +111 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main passes `cfg?.file` unchanged and ignores `appendLogLine()` false returns, and the PR body includes after-fix terminal proof; I did not run a mutable repro in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can remove or migration-back the stricter schema validation while preserving the useful runtime logging fix.

target.title: fix(logging): surface silent file-write failures and reject empty paths
target.body:
## Summary

When `logging.file` resolves to a path that cannot be written to (empty string, whitespace-only, directory, trailing slash, read-only filesystem, etc.), `appendLogLine` currently swallows the error and returns `false`. The caller drops the boolean return, so the gateway keeps running while no log lines are ever persisted. This is inconsistent with `rotateLogFile`, which at least emits one stderr warning on failure.

Three small fixes:

1. **Schema** (`src/config/zod-schema.ts:587`): `file: z.string().min(1).optional()` — rejects empty strings at config validation time.
2. **resolveSettings** (`src/logging/logger.ts:559`): Trim whitespace and fall back to the default rolling path when the path is empty or whitespace-only — same behavior as when `logging.file` is unset.
3. **Transport** (`src/logging/logger.ts:649-654`): Emit a one-time stderr warning (`[openclaw] log file write failed; continuing without file log ...`) on persistent write failure, mirroring the existing `rotateLogFile` failure path. The flag resets on the next successful write so fixing the path clears the warning.

## Linked context

N/A (no existing issue)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `logging.file` with empty/whitespace/read-only paths silently drops all log writes with zero user-visible feedback.
- **Real environment tested:** Node 22.19, Linux x86_64, local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` main (e84d68e794).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  node --import tsx /tmp/verify-logging-fix.mjs
  ```
  Script imports the patched `logger.ts` and verifies: empty path fallback, whitespace trimming, and stderr warning emission.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture of `node` runtime verification (copied live output):

  ```
  === Test 1: empty logging.file ===

    Input: file=""
    Resolved file: /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-17.log
    PASS

  === Test 2: whitespace-only logging.file ===

    Input: file="   "
    Resolved file: /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-17.log
    PASS

  === Test 3: trailing whitespace trimming ===

    Input: file="/tmp/test-openclaw.log  "
    Resolved file: /tmp/test-openclaw.log
    PASS

  === Test 4: stderr warning on write failure ===

  [openclaw] log file write failed; continuing without file log file=/tmp/openclaw-log-fail-proof-1ab1fbc7-2810-405e-bed0-5ad7665a618b/openclaw.log (will retry silently; check path/permissions)
    stderr warning: [openclaw] log file write failed; continuing without file log file=...
    PASS

  === ALL PASSED ===
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Empty/whitespace paths now fall back to the default rolling log path (instead of passing `""` to `fs.appendFileSync`). Write failures now emit a one-time stderr warning, matching the `rotateLogFile` behavior.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end test with a real read-only container filesystem (covered by the chmod-based write-failure test).

## Tests and validation

```
$ pnpm test src/logging/log-file-silent-failure.test.ts src/config/logging-file-empty.test.ts src/logging/log-file-size-cap.test.ts
✓ Test Files  3 passed (3)
✓ Tests      10 passed (10)
```

## Risk checklist

- [x] Does this change affect user-visible behavior? **Yes** — users who previously had empty or whitespace-only `logging.file` will now get the default rolling log path instead of silent write failures; unwritable paths now produce a one-time stderr warning.
- [x] Does this change affect configuration? **Yes** — empty string for `logging.file` is now rejected by schema validation (`min(1)`). This is backwards-compatible: empty paths previously produced silent failures; they now produce a clear validation error.
- [x] Does this change affect security? **No**
- [x] Does this change affect plugins or providers? **No**
- [x] Does this change affect docs? **No**
- [x] Is this change backwards-compatible? **Yes** — all previously-valid non-empty paths continue to work identically. Empty paths that previously silently failed now either fail validation or fall back to default.
target.author: miorbnli

commands.author: Alix-007

target.title: fix(gateway): remove device-backed node pairings
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88488.

`node.list` can surface Android/phone nodes backed by `devices/paired.json`, but `node.pair.remove` previously removed only legacy `nodes/paired.json` records. The visible node therefore remained authorized and reappeared.

This PR:

- removes the effective `node` role from the canonical paired-device store;
- deletes node-only device rows while preserving unrelated roles/tokens on mixed-role devices;
- clears matching pending repair requests and any legacy node pairing;
- applies the same device-management authorization policy as `device.pair.remove`;
- invalidates node-role clients before the success response and disconnects after it;
- places `node.pair.remove` behind the post-connect credential-mutation barrier;
- emits redacted trusted security events for allowed and denied node-role removal;
- documents device-backed and mixed-role removal semantics.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Device-backed nodes shown by `node.list` can be removed durably; merged legacy/device records are both cleared; mixed-role devices keep the operator role; removed node credentials cannot pipeline another request; allow/deny decisions are auditable without exposing raw device IDs or tokens.

Real environment tested: Local Node checkout plus AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_a5289b2e7c04` (`swift-lobster`, c7a.32xlarge). Tests drive the real pairing stores, production Gateway handlers, client invalidation/disconnect ordering, diagnostic-event stream, and post-connect mutation barrier.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs +  src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.test.ts +  src/gateway/server-methods/devices.test.ts +  src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts +  src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts +  src/infra/node-pairing.test.ts

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed runtime and test files>
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search
```

Evidence after fix: Local focused proof passed 211 tests. AWS Crabbox run `run_bea1e028e58e` passed 226 exact-worktree tests on Linux. Disk readback proves node-only rows disappear from `devices/paired.json`, merged rows disappear from both stores, and mixed-role rows retain only the operator role/token. Handler assertions prove invalidation occurs before response and disconnect after response. Trusted security events contain hashed device identifiers and `role: node`, never the raw ID. Type-aware oxlint is clean. Final autoreview is clean with 0.96 confidence.

Observed result after fix: `node.pair.remove` returns the normalized node ID, removes the effective node credential, clears runtime state, emits the removal event, and blocks queued credential use. Non-admin device-token attempts against mixed-role rows fail without mutation and emit a redacted denial event.

What was not tested: No GUI/screenshot path and no physical Android device. The changed contract is Gateway pairing/storage/authentication behavior and is covered through production handlers with real on-disk stores.

## Tests

- AWS Crabbox `run_bea1e028e58e`: 226 passed.
- Local focused suite: 211 passed.
- Type-aware oxlint: clean.
- Fresh autoreview: clean.
